mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding labor income and aggregate consumption demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Sharpe of the PAYEMS momentum signal is below 0.2 over any 3-year rolling window, or the signal produces positive returns in less than 55% of months.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 3.6% signals slack accumulation and rising recession probability, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations are marked down.
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE exceeds 3.6%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the year-over-year growth rate of CPIAUCSL exceeds the effective FEDFUNDS rate, deeply negative real policy rates force accelerated monetary tightening, compressing equity risk premiums and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS shows a Rank IC above 0.02 with positive sign over any 2-year rolling window, or the signal fails to outperform the single-series FEDFUNDS model by more than 50bps annualized.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery, quarterly real GDP growth above trend signals durable demand normalization and corporate revenue rebound, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Following quarters with annualized GDPC1 growth above 2%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances, or the cumulative return spread versus low-growth quarters is not positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production reflects rising manufacturing capacity utilization and positive operating leverage for industrial firms, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward 5-day forward returns following month-over-month INDPRO increases are not significantly greater than returns following decreases (t-test p > 0.05) over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A 5% or greater decline in retail sales over three months signals a contraction in consumer spending and broad demand deterioration, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When RSAFS declines exceed 5% over 3 months, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the mean triggered return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising unemployment and falling retail sales captures a consumer-driven recessionary dynamic where labor income contraction reinforces demand weakness, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint UNRATE-RSAFS signal produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction term fails to add incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.10) relative to the individual series in a rolling 3-year regression.

mechanism_narrative: An effective federal funds rate above 3.5% reflects a restrictive monetary policy stance that rapidly increases short-term discount rates and corporate borrowing costs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.5%, subsequent 5-day S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 55% of weeks, or the mean triggered return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).
